name: YouTube Trailers
description:
Resolves YouTube trailers server-side with yt-dlp and stream-copy remuxes
them to AVPlayer-native fMP4 HLS, so tvOS / AVKit clients (Apple TV) play one
clean URL with zero on-device extraction — for both in-app trailers and the
Top Shelf. No re-encoding; resolution is cached and served as a live HLS
bundle. Includes a dashboard config page (tool paths, format selector, extra
yt-dlp arguments, cache location, yt-dlp version) and a daily prune scheduled
task that bounds the cache by age and LRU size cap so it never grows unbounded.
overview: Server-side YouTube trailers as AVPlayer-native HLS for tvOS clients.

versions.changelog: Finite VOD playlist for full-screen clients (?complete=true) so AVKit shows a real scrubber instead of the live UI.
Negative-cache failed trailer builds so repeat requests fail fast (404 in ms) instead of re-timing-out, enabling a quick client-side fallback to the next trailer.

Proxy now routes the ffmpeg fetch via environment variables, fixing servers whose ffmpeg ignores the -http_proxy option (e.g. Jellyfin's Windows 7.x build). The proxy bypasses a bad network route to a Google CDN edge end to end.

ffmpeg now reconnects on transient network errors instead of failing a build when the server's connection to Google's CDN times out. New Proxy setting that applies to both yt-dlp and the actual ffmpeg fetch, so geo-blocked trailers work end to end (a proxy in yt-dlp args alone only covered resolution).

The plugin now downloads and maintains its own official yt-dlp standalone binary for your server's OS/architecture (Linux x64/arm64/armv7, macOS, Windows) — no manual install and no Python required, so it works out of the box. Set a yt-dlp path only to pin a system copy. Config page adds a "Download / update yt-dlp" button and shows the detected version.

Server-side YouTube trailer resolution (yt-dlp) stream-copy remuxed to AVPlayer-native fMP4 HLS, for in-app trailers and the Top Shelf — zero on-device extraction. Dashboard config page (tool paths, format selector, extra yt-dlp arguments, cache directory, yt-dlp version) and a daily prune task that bounds the cache by age and LRU size.
